items.prompt: How many independent boolean features can be represented directly by a w-bit mask when each feature owns one bit?

feedback.reason: One bit stores one independent true-or-false feature.
feedback.details: The same w positions jointly represent 2^w different combinations of those features.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.two_w_features: 2^w is the number of possible states, not the number of independent bit positions.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.w_squared: The mask contains only w positions.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.one_feature: Each bit can encode a separate boolean.

items.prompt: A state mask uses bit i to mean resource i is acquired. Which expression tests whether resource i is acquired?

options.text: (state AND (1 shifted left by i)) is nonzero.
(state OR (1 shifted left by i)) is nonzero.
state shifted left by i is nonzero.

feedback.reason: A one-hot mask extracts the selected membership bit.
feedback.details: The result is nonzero exactly when the feature is present.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.or_nonzero: OR would make the bit one even when it was previously absent.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.state_equal_i: The numeric state can contain many resources.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.shift_state: Shifting does not isolate the selected resource.

items.prompt: Which operation combines two feature masks into a state containing every feature present in either mask?

feedback.reason: OR represents set union over boolean feature positions.
feedback.details: Every bit present in either operand becomes present in the result.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bitwise_and: AND keeps only features present in both.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bitwise_xor: XOR removes features present in both.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.addition: Carries can corrupt neighboring feature positions.

items.prompt: Which operation produces a mask containing only features present in both state masks?

feedback.reason: AND is set intersection over one-bit feature memberships.
feedback.details: A feature remains one only when both input states contain it.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bitwise_or: OR keeps features present in either state.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bitwise_xor: XOR keeps features present in exactly one operand.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.complement: Complement inverts membership rather than intersecting.

items.prompt: A transition releases every resource selected by mask released. Which update matches the contract?

options.text: state = state AND complement(released), using the declared width.

feedback.reason: Release requires selected membership bits to become zero while all others remain unchanged.
feedback.details: Width-limited complement supplies zeros at the released positions and ones elsewhere.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.toggle_mask: This would acquire a resource that was not currently held.

constraints: Complement is limited to the state-mask width.

items.prompt: When do two bitmask-encoded boolean states represent exactly the same feature set?

options.text: When their masks are bitwise equal.
When they contain the same number of set bits.
When their highest set bit matches.

feedback.reason: The bit-position mapping is a canonical state representation.
feedback.details: Every membership position must agree for states to be equal.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.same_popcount: Different feature sets can have equal size.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.and_nonzero: That proves only some shared feature.

items.prompt: Which expression tests whether feature sets a and b are disjoint?

feedback.reason: Any shared feature would survive the AND.
feedback.details: A zero intersection means no bit position is set in both states.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.or_zero: That requires both states to be empty.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.xor_zero: That means the states are equal.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.and_equals_a: That means a is a subset of b.

items.prompt: Which expression tests whether every feature in mask a is also present in mask b?

feedback.reason: Intersecting a with b should preserve all of a when a is a subset of b.
feedback.details: Any missing required bit would be cleared in the AND result.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.or_equals_a: That means b is a subset of a.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.xor_equals_a: XOR does not directly express subset inclusion.

items.prompt: A task requires all flags in required to be enabled in state, while additional flags are allowed. Which test is correct?

feedback.reason: The test extracts required positions and verifies that every one is present.
feedback.details: Unrelated state bits do not affect the comparison.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.any_required: This proves only at least one required flag.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.exact_state: This incorrectly rejects additional allowed flags.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.or_zero: A nonempty required mask makes this impossible.

items.prompt: A system needs 70 independent boolean features, but the chosen primitive mask exposes only 64 usable bits. What is the correct design response?

options.text: Use a wider arbitrary-precision or multiword representation, or another explicit state structure.
Map multiple unrelated features to the same bit.
Shift a 64-bit one by positions 64 through 69 and assume portability.

feedback.reason: One-bit-per-feature encoding requires at least as many usable positions as features.
feedback.details: Changing representation is safer than introducing collisions or relying on undefined shift assumptions.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.ignore_six: The representation would no longer encode the contract.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.shift_beyond: Oversized shift behavior is model-dependent and cannot create extra storage positions.

items.prompt: Which problem states are natural candidates for one-bit-per-feature encoding? Select all that apply.

options.text: Which members of a small fixed universe have been visited.
Which small indexed resources are currently used.
An arbitrary unbounded count for every feature.

feedback.reason: Bitmask state is suitable for bounded collections of independent booleans.
feedback.details: Counts or large values need more than one membership bit per field.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.arbitrary_large_counts: One bit cannot store an unbounded count.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.visited_small_set: Each universe member maps to one membership bit.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.used_resources: Each resource can own a bit.

items.prompt: Which set-style operations are represented correctly by bitwise operators? Select all that apply.

feedback.reason: Boolean membership algebra maps directly to OR, AND, and XOR.
feedback.details: Set difference instead uses AND with a width-limited complement.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.difference_add: Carries do not represent membership difference.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.symmetric_xor: Bits present in exactly one state remain.

items.prompt: Which requirements make a bit-position mapping reliable? Select all that apply.

options.text: Each feature has one unique bit position.
The mapping remains stable anywhere masks are compared or persisted.
The available width and valid positions are documented.
Feature labels must be sorted numerically.

feedback.reason: The mask is only canonical when every position has one stable meaning inside a declared width.
feedback.details: Sorting labels is optional and does not define correctness.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.value_sorted: The mapping can use any documented one-to-one assignment.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.width_documented: Capacity and complement behavior depend on width.

items.prompt: Which transitions preserve unselected feature bits? Select all that apply.

options.text: Set selected features with state OR mask.
Clear selected features with state AND complement(mask).
Toggle selected features with state XOR mask.
Replace state with state AND mask.

feedback.reason: A state transition should alter only positions explicitly selected by the transition mask.
feedback.details: Direct extraction is useful for testing but not for preserving unrelated state.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.clear_and_not: Unselected positions meet ones in the complemented mask.

items.prompt: Which tests are important for bitmask state representation? Select all that apply.

options.text: The zero mask with no features enabled.
Two different features enabled separately and together.

feedback.reason: State tests should cover empty, full, and independence properties.
feedback.details: A single ordinary feature cannot prove that the mapping is collision-free.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.only_one_middle: This does not test collisions or width endpoints.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.full_state: It tests capacity and complement boundaries.

items.prompt: Arrange the design process for a bitmask state model.

elements.text: List the boolean features that must be represented.
Assign one unique zero-based bit position to each feature.
Choose a representation wide enough for every assigned position.
Document membership meaning and legal state operations.

feedback.reason: Representation design begins with semantic fields and ends with an explicit positional contract.
feedback.details: Choosing a word before counting features can create silent width overflow.

items.prompt: Arrange the steps for encoding a selected feature collection into one mask.

elements.text: Look up each selected feature's assigned bit position.
OR the corresponding one-hot mask into state.
Test that all selected features are present and no unintended positions were used.

feedback.reason: Encoding accumulates independent feature memberships with OR.
feedback.details: Verification catches mapping and width mistakes before the state is consumed.

items.prompt: Arrange a transition that acquires features add, releases features remove, and then verifies required features.

elements.text: Update state = state AND complement(remove) within the width.
Check that the extracted bits equal required.

feedback.reason: Set and clear transitions update distinct selected positions, after which the required-state postcondition is tested.
feedback.details: If add and remove overlap, the stated ordering makes removal win; a different policy must be explicit.

constraints: Complement is limited to the declared state width.

items.prompt: On one fixed-width machine word, a state transition performs a constant number of OR, AND, XOR, and comparison operations. Select time and auxiliary-space complexity.
constraints: The feature count fits inside the fixed word.

feedback.reason: A constant number of primitive fixed-word operations is constant time.
feedback.details: The representation remains compact only while all features fit the chosen word.

items.prompt: A dynamic-programming table stores one constant-size value for every state of n independent boolean features. Select initialization time and table space.
constraints: Every one of the 2^n masks receives one table entry.

feedback.reason: A compact encoding of one state does not make the full state space small.
feedback.details: Enumerating or storing every combination remains exponential in the number of boolean features.
